body.p1: A block explorer is a public chain viewer. It helps users search transaction IDs, block hashes, block heights, and addresses. It does not replace your wallet backup and it cannot recover private keys.
body.p2: Address search shows public chain activity for that address when the explorer index supports it. Address search does not prove who owns an address, and it does not reveal private keys.
body.p3: The current site includes an explorer at /explorer/. The explorer.hobbyhashcoin.com hostname forwards visitors to the explorer home.
desc.block_height: The position of a block in the chain.
desc.confirmations: Blocks added after the transaction's block.
desc.difficulty: The current work target miners must beat to find a block.
desc.mempool: Unconfirmed transactions waiting to be mined into a block.
hero.lead: Search HOBC blocks, transactions, and addresses with real chain data only.
hero.title: Explorer Guide
meta.description: How to use the HOBC public explorer.
meta.title: Explorer Guide
step.block.1: Search by block hash or block height.
step.block.2: Check block time, transaction count, and confirmations.
step.block.3: For mining, compare pool block records with explorer block records when both are available.
step.tx.1: Copy the transaction ID from your wallet, pool, or service.
step.tx.3: Paste the transaction ID into the search box.
step.tx.4: Review block height, confirmations, and transaction details when available.
